posts.body:
Hi everyone, please, help a rookie!

So let's say I want to buy a card that costs 5 emeralds.
I have 5 emerald cards and 2 emerald gems. 
In this situation do I 
1) pay with my 5 emerald cards and keep my 2 emerald gems
2) pay with my 2 emerald gems plus 3 emeralds cards?

In other words, do I always have to use gems first and then cards?
Or I can decide to keep however many gems I want?

Thanks a lot, I love Splendor and Boardgamegeek!

You decide what to do.  I recommend using your 5 emerald cards and saving your 2 emerald gems in order to buy a card requiring 6 or 7 emeralds on a later turn.  Remember:  you expend gems but you keep cards.

Considering that you cannot hold more than 10 gems from the end of one turn to the next, you can always get rid of any emerald gems that you feel are unneeded when you take 2 or 3 new gems from the supply. You can take more gems putting you over 10, but you have to get rid of enough to get back down to 10 before your turn ends.  In other words, let's say that I have 10 gems including those 2 emerald gems.  I take 2 ruby gems from the supply, raising my total to 12.  To get back down to 10, I return the 2 emerald gems to the supply.   That's my turn.

For me, I'd go with keeping the 2 emerald gems and just using the 5 emerald cards.  It's what we call a 'free card' in our Splendor games because you are actually spending nothing.

If you like Splendor, check out Gizmos.

That makes sense, and that's what I thought!
Thank you for confirming that and I appreciate your fast reply because we played yesterday and we're going to play right now!
Hello from Japan and have a great day!

Relevant rules quote:
The bonuses a player has from development cards acquired
on previous turns provide discounts on the purchase of new
cards. Each bonus of a given color is equal to a token of that
color.
Thus, if a player has 2 blue bonuses and wants to purchase a
card which costs 2 blue tokens and 1 green token, the player
must only spend 1 green token.
If a player has enough development cards (and therefore
bonuses), they can even purchase a card without spending any
tokens.
Yep, some people forget to read the rules :-(.

Eldard wrote:

You decide what to do.  I recommend using your 5 emerald cards and saving your 2 emerald gems in order to buy a card requiring 6 or 7 emeralds on a later turn. 

I don't think the rules mention any kind of choice in that matter.
Card bonuses give discounts and the remainder must be paid with tokens.
TBH I can't think of a situation where it would be beneficial to voluntarily overspend.

Just to be clear:  You don't turn in your cards once you use the gems on them.  They are permanently in your possession.

You use the gems on them many times.

Your "pay with my cards" terminology made me wonder if you got that.

You're right, "pay with cards" sounds like I return the cards.
No, the cards stay in my "wallet" forever and I use them as gem value repeatedly throughout the game. 
Thank you!
